questions.text:
A table shows types of vehicles passing a town centre. If the table shows 5 vans and 3 lorries, how many more vans than lorries passed?
(Based on a table showing vehicles: Cars, Motorcycles, Vans, Lorries, Bicycles)

A table shows the heights of 9 pupils. Ethan is listed with a height of 142 cm in the table. Prema weighs 34 kg. Who is the tallest?
(Based on a table of pupils' heights and masses)

A table shows the number of boys and girls travelling to school by bus or MRT. If there are 12 girls total and 8 boys travel by bus, how many more girls than boys are there in total?
(Based on a table of boys and girls travelling by bus or MRT)

A line graph shows the mass of newspapers collected over 5 weeks. The class collected 60 kg in the 2nd week and 100 kg in the 1st week. What was the mass collected in the 2nd week?
(Based on: Old Newspapers Collected Over 5 Weeks — bar/line graph)

A line graph shows health checkups conducted over 6 months (Jan to Jun). Which month had the most health checkups?
(Based on: Health Checkups Conducted Over 6 Months — line graph)

A line graph shows temperature recorded over a 12-hour period in Hong Kong. The temperature at 10 a.m. was 28°C and at 2 p.m. was 34°C. What was the temperature recorded at 10 a.m.?
(Based on: Temperature in Hong Kong — 6am to 6pm line graph)

A line graph shows the hours of bicycle rental from March to July. June had 6,000 hours and July had 5,500 hours. Which month had the most hours of bicycle rental?
(Based on: Hours of Bicycle Rental Over 5 Months — line graph)

(Based on: Temperature of Object heated over 5 minutes — line graph)

A table shows recyclables collected by four classes. Class 4A collected 48 kg, Class 4B collected 36 kg, Class 4C collected 42 kg, and Class 4D collected 30 kg. How much did class 4B collect?
(Based on: Recyclables collected by four classes — table)

A table shows pupils' scores and the number of pupils who obtained each score. If 8 pupils obtained a score of 10 and 5 pupils obtained a score of 15, how many pupils obtained a score of 10?
(Based on: Score vs Number of Pupils table)

A line graph shows earnings at Mrs Isa's fruit stall from January to May. She earned $1,000 in January, $2,000 in February, $1,500 in March, $1,500 in April, and $3,000 in May. How much did she earn in March?
(Based on: Earnings at Mrs Isa's Fruit Stall — line graph)

A bar graph shows movie DVD sales over 5 days. Wednesday had 60 DVDs, Thursday had 80 DVDs, Friday had 40 DVDs, Saturday had 100 DVDs, and Sunday had 120 DVDs. Which day had the most DVD sales?
(Based on: Movie DVD Sales Over 5 Days — bar graph)

A line graph shows the number of books loaned out by a school library over four days. If the graph shows 200 books on Monday, 250 on Tuesday, 300 on Wednesday, and 200 on Thursday, on which day were the most books loaned?
(Based on: Library books loaned over four days — line graph)

The table shows types of vehicles: Cars (20), Motorcycles (15), Vans (8), Lorries (5), Bicycles (12). How many vehicles passed the town centre in total?
(Based on: Vehicles passing town centre between 9.30 a.m. and 9.45 a.m. on Sunday)

(Based on: Temperature in Hong Kong line graph)

Express 6:40 a.m. using the 24-hour clock.
(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — Express times using 24-hour clock)

Express 10:05 p.m. using the 24-hour clock.
(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — Express times using 24-hour clock)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — Express times using 12-hour clock)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — Express times using 12-hour clock)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — time duration calculations)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — end time calculations)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — musical start time problem)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — baking start time in 24-hour clock)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Review — 18-hour later calculation)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Practice — walking time calculation)

(Based on: Chapter 15 Practice — movie start time)

A New Year party started at 22:30 on Friday and ended at 01:15 the next day. How long was the party?
(Based on: Chapter 15 Practice — party duration)

Rafi walked from Town A to Town E. He left Town A at 09:00 and reached Town B at 10:00, Town C at 10:42, and Town D at 11:56. How long did he take to walk from Town A to Town B?
(Based on: Chapter 15 Practice — walking between towns)
A table shows Class 4A collected 48 kg of recyclables and Class 4D collected 30 kg. Class 4D needs to collect _____ kg more to match Class 4A.
The line graph shows that Mrs Isa earned $1,500 in March and $1,500 in April. The two months where she earned the same amount were _____ and _____.
A line graph shows the total mass of newspapers collected in the 1st week was 100 kg and in the 3rd week was 80 kg. The total mass collected over the 3rd week was _____ kg.
The table shows pupils' scores. The most common score was 10 (obtained by 8 pupils). The least common score was _____ (obtained by 3 pupils).
The line graph shows bicycle rental hours in March was 3,000 and in April was 4,000. The increase from March to April was _____ hours.
A bar graph shows DVD sales: Wed 60, Thu 80, Fri 40, Sat 100, Sun 120. The total DVD sales for the whole week were _____ DVDs.
The line graph shows health checkups in March was 55 and in May was 35. The difference in the number of checkups between March and May was _____.
The table shows vehicles passing a town centre: 5 vans and 3 lorries. There were _____ more vans than lorries.
The line graph shows the temperature of an object in the 3rd minute was 100°C and in the 5th minute was 180°C. The increase in temperature from the 3rd to the 5th minute was _____ °C.
Mrs Isa's earnings from January to May were $1,000, $2,000, $1,500, $1,500, and $3,000 respectively. She earned twice as much in May as she did in _____, because $3,000 is twice $1,500.
Express 3:15 p.m. using the 24-hour clock: _____.
Express 2330 using the 12-hour clock: _____.
Rafi left Town A at 09:00 and reached Town B at 10:00. He took _____ hour(s) to walk from Town A to Town B.
A mathematics test began at 11:35 and lasted 55 minutes. Joy finished the test at _____: _____ (24-hour clock).
Gail left her house at 06:52 and reached the bus stop at 07:15. She took _____ minutes to walk from her house to the bus stop.
A movie started at 21:45 and ended at 00:36 the next day. The movie lasted _____ hours _____ minutes.
The heritage trail schedule shows: Asian Civilisations Museum from 10:00 to 10:50, then Bus ride to The Arts House from 10:50 to _____, and The Arts House from _____ to 12:00.

A dental clinic is open from 09:30 to 13:00 in the morning and from 14:30 to 17:30 in the afternoon, and from 19:00 to 21:00 in the evening. The clinic is open for a total of _____ hours on a weekday.
Rafi walked from Town C (10:42) to Town D (11:56). The time taken from Town C to Town D was _____ hours _____ minutes.
Jenny's birthday was on 12 October at 17:45. Her sister's celebration was 18 hours later, which was on _____ October at _____: _____.

The bar graph shows movie DVD sales over 5 days: Wednesday 60, Thursday 80, Friday 40, Saturday 100, Sunday 120. Which line graph correctly shows this data? Explain why the other line graph is incorrect.
questions.answer: The correct line graph should show a sharp rise from Friday (40) to Sunday (120) with a dip on Friday. The incorrect graph would show a different pattern or wrong order of days.

questions.text: The line graph shows Mrs Isa's earnings from January to May as $1,000, $2,000, $1,500, $1,500, and $3,000. During which 1-month interval was the increase in earnings the greatest? Explain how you found your answer.
questions.answer: The greatest increase was from April to May, when earnings rose from $1,500 to $3,000, an increase of $1,500. This is larger than all other month-to-month changes.

questions.text: A concert started at 9:30 p.m. on Friday and lasted 2 hours 45 minutes. Show how to find the concert end time using the 24-hour clock, and give the final answer.
questions.answer: Starting at 9:30 p.m. = 21:30. Adding 2 hours gives 23:30, then adding 45 minutes gives 00:15 the next day (Saturday). So the concert ended at 00:15 on Saturday, expressed as 00:15.

questions.text: Rafi walked from Town A to Town E. He left at 09:00, reached Town B at 10:00, Town C at 10:42, Town D at 11:56, and Town E at 18:11. He took 7 hours 15 minutes to walk from Town D to Town E. Show how to calculate the time taken for each segment: A→B, B→C, C→D.
questions.answer: A to B: 10:00 − 09:00 = 1 hour. B to C: 10:42 − 10:00 = 42 minutes. C to D: 11:56 − 10:42 = 1 hour 14 minutes.

questions.text: A New Year party started at 22:30 on Friday and ended at 01:15 the next day. Show all the steps to find how long the party lasted. Explain why you need to add 24 hours to 01:15 before subtracting.
questions.answer: First convert 01:15 (Saturday morning) to 24-hour format past midnight of Friday: 01:15 + 24 hours = 25:15. Then subtract the start time: 25:15 − 22:30 = 2 hours 45 minutes. The party lasted 2 hours 45 minutes.
